info.parenttitle: A3.2 Introduction to structural systems (HL only)

markdown:
# Static and Dynamic Forces in Structural Systems


<definition term="Forces">**Factors affecting strength** when **compressing, pushing, pulling, twisting, or squeezing** an object.</definition>

Forces can be:

1. **Static** – loads that do not move (e.g. weight of a building)
2. **Dynamic** – loads that **change over time** (e.g. wind, people walking, vehicles)

## Static Forces


<definition term="Static Force">A **stationary force** acting on a structure, including its **own weight** and the **load it carries**.</definition>

1. **Static forces** are **constant** or **slowly changing** forces acting on a structure.
2. They include:
  1. **Compression** – squashing force
  2. **Tension** – pulling force
  3. **Torsion** – twisting force
  4. **Bending** – curving under load
  5. **Shear** – sliding force

### Compression


<definition term="Compression (force)">A **force that squeezes** a material or causes it to **shorten**.</definition>

<callout type="note">Common in: **columns**, **legs of a chair**, **arches**</callout>

![Compression forces push inward, squashing the material (corrosionpedia)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/73f9b4a4015454f5da32dbd426ffcba8d2782ea0-1000x600.png)

### Tension


<definition term="Tension (force)">A **pulling or stretching force** that acts to **extend** a material.</definition>

<callout type="note">Common in: **shafts**, **door handles**, **bike pedals**</callout>

![Tension forces pull outward, stretching the material (jove)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/f87883006ca520e7502cd3282784aecc51a44490-1920x1080.png)

### Torsion


<definition term="Torsion (force)">A twisting force.</definition>

<callout type="note">Common in: **shafts**, **door handles**, **bike pedals**</callout>

![Torsion forces twist a material around its axis (structures explained)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/8e3a5845ffbebfabed0ec083c53ae80cfb34be2b-1917x1080.png)

### Bending


<definition term="Bending (force)">A **load applied at a distance** from a **fixed point**, causing a material to **bend**.</definition>

<callout type="note">**Bending Moment (M)**:** Force (F) × Distance (d)**

* **Force (F)**: The **perpendicular force** applied to the beam.
* **Distance (d)**: The **perpendicular distance** from the force to the point of calculation (**lever arm** or **moment arm**).</callout>

<callout type="note">Common in: **beams**, **floorboards**, **bookshelves**</callout>

![Bending force causes compression on top and tension underneath the beam (the gund company)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/eec4323c1cd1e04067d19b5f01bb628125a64e23-336x150.png)

### Shear


<definition term="Shear (force)">Two **equal and opposite forces** acting on an object that are **not aligned**, creating a **cutting effect**.</definition>

<callout type="note">Common in: **scissors**, **riveted joints**, **bolts in wind loads**</callout>

![Opposing forces acting in different directions, causing sliding at the joint (engineer excel)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/34ba3e0fe099b92df7a0687895c906621d851594-2000x2000.png)

<callout type="note">**Static forces **are often **predictable **and can be calculated during the design phase.</callout>

## Dynamic Forces


<definition term="Dynamic Force">A **moving force** acting on structures, often caused by **wind, vehicles**, or other **external motion**.</definition>

Dynamic forces **change over time**, often suddenly or repeatedly. These include:

1. **Wind Loads** – e.g. gusts hitting a tall building
2. **Seismic Loads** – e.g. ground shaking during an earthquake
3. **Impact Loads** – e.g. a car hitting a barrier or bridge

<callout type="note">**Dynamic forces **are often **unpredictable **and require **special consideration **in design.</callout>

### Static vs Dynamic Forces


| Type of Force | Description | Examples |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Static | Constant, unchanging | Building weight, furniture resting on a floor |
| Dynamic | Changing over time or location | Wind, footsteps, traffic, earthquakes |

<callout type="example">**Skyscrapers**

* **Static Forces**: Weight of the building materials.
* **Dynamic Forces**: Wind and earthquakes.

![Wind effects on a skyscraper (simscale)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/d5d74eda569645cf3aff3364c9cec15908ae2887-1104x750.png)</callout>
